title: Significant Problem, Public Value, and Durable Need · Guided Vertical Slice
summary: A guided product team extends significance and public-value case while preserving every earlier problem, accessibility, security, testing, operations, and recovery requirement. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of significance and public-value case while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Triangulate direct research, domain artifacts, credible data, existing services, and counterevidence to establish problem significance.
Bound affected populations, contexts, frequency, severity, unequal burden, and who is missing from available evidence.
Study current services, manual workarounds, nonuse, competitors, public infrastructure, and build-nothing options.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Significance Evidence
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The workshop variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Significance Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for triangulate direct research, domain artifacts, credible data, existing services, and counterevidence to establish problem significance. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Significance Evidence, keep Significance Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The workshop variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Significance Evidence condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Secure Software Development Framework — Controls prepared organizations, protected software, well-secured production, vulnerability response, provenance, verification, and release evidence.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Significance Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Significance Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop Significance Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The workshop variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

options.text: Novel technology makes the addressed problem significant. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Significance Evidence.

For commit to the mission model, apply Significance Evidence without its stated preconditions, omit the significance and public-value case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Significance Evidence for significance and public-value case.
State the Significance Evidence preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

content.text: The Durability Change structural check is only one gate. significance and public-value case must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Durability Change condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Durability Change
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Durability Change: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A novel technical demonstration lacks durable need evidence, reachable affected people, current alternatives, or a causal route to public value. The workshop variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Durability Change.
steps.why: Retrieving Durability Change through the concrete significance and public-value case case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Durability Change, keep Durability Change connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test whether the need persists under policy, market, technology, organizational, and behavior change across the project horizon.
